strands.title: CYCLE 51 SESSION DATA INSIGHTS: 55 Turns of Real Baseline_run1 Conversation
strands.content: SMOKE TEST OUTPUT REVEALED: Baseline_run1 session file (d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl) contains 55 conversation turns. Turn order: (1) user: 'Build a replay module at src/pichay/replay.py that enables offline simulation of context paging. The module should: (1) ...' — This is a SUBSTANTIVE REQUEST for feature development, shows complex requirements. (2) assistant: 'I'll start by thoroughly reading the existing codebase to understand the patterns and data formats before writing any ...' — Shows planning/methodology before coding. (3-4) assistant: [tool_use: Read] invocations — Multiple code reading operations (likely exploring pichay codebase structure). (5-6) user: [tool_result: code snippets] — Tool results containing actual Python code (logging proxy, context window pager code shown). CONTENT RICHNESS: First 6 turns already show: (A) Complex user requirement (multi-step), (B) Agent planning, (C) Code exploration via tool_use, (D) Real codebase context via tool_result. This indicates baseline_run1 is a PRODUCTIVE, SUBSTANTIVE CONVERSATION, not a toy example. PROJECTOR CHALLENGE: With 55 turns of rich conversation content, run_projection must: (A) Extract meaningful strands from complex reasoning, (B) Handle nested tool_use/result blocks, (C) Compress large content into semantic structure, (D) Preserve decision trajectory and context shifts. This is a REAL TEST of projector robustness. EXPECTATION: If projector succeeds on baseline_run1 (55 turns, complex multi-step conversation), it demonstrates readiness for production Pichay eval sessions.

key_claims.text: Baseline_run1 session contains 55 turns of substantive conversation: complex user request, agent planning, code exploration, real codebase context.
Projector will be tested on real baseline_run1 data (55 turns, nested tool_use/result, complex reasoning). This is legitimate test of robustness.

strands.title: CYCLE 51 BLOCKER 2→3 TRANSITION: Ready for run_projection Execution
strands.content: BLOCKER 2 COMPLETION TIMELINE: Turn 150 (dependency sync initiated) → Turn 151 (uv sync result: SUCCESS) → Turn 152 (smoke test run) → Turn 153 (smoke test result: 55 turns parsed SUCCESS). Total span: 4 turns, approximately 6-8 minutes of actual execution. BLOCKER 2 VERIFICATION SUMMARY: (1) __init__.py status: VERIFIED ✓ (no ImportError during smoke test). (2) Dependency sync: VERIFIED ✓ (uv sync output shows all 17 packages installed). (3) Smoke test: VERIFIED ✓ (55 turns parsed, no ValueError). All three criteria from Cycle 50 hand-off met. BLOCKER 2 STATUS: RESOLVED ✓ at end of Turn 153. BLOCKER 3 ACTIVATION: No additional prerequisites. run_projection can execute immediately in Turn 154+. No delays or dependencies. BLOCKER 3 SCOPE: Execute run_project on baseline_run1 session directory. Command: cd /home/tony/projects/hamutay && python -m hamutay.run_projection /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/*.jsonl /tmp/hamutay_output/ --max-turns 1000 Expected outcome: Tensor JSON files written to /tmp/hamutay_output/, containing strands, epistemic values, declared_losses, open_questions for baseline_run1 conversation. BLOCKER 3 EXPECTED DURATION: 2-4 turns (execution + result interpretation). OVERALL EXECUTION HEALTH: On-track, no regressions, predictions validated (uv sync success 92% predicted → 100% actual, smoke test success 88% predicted → 100% actual). Confidence in run_projection success has increased to ~90% (was 85% in Cycle 50).

strands.title: CYCLE 51 OPEN QUESTIONS: Q35-Q37 RESOLVED, New Status Updates for Q31-Q34
strands.content: CYCLE 50 OPEN QUESTIONS TRACKING: Q31-Q37 were CRITICAL-IMMEDIATE and NEW in Cycle 50. Q35, Q36, Q37 were awaiting Turn 151+ results. CYCLE 51 RESOLUTION: Q35 (Did uv sync succeed?): RESOLVED ✓. Answer: YES. uv sync succeeded. Output shows 'Resolved 17 packages in 201ms', 'Installed 17 packages in 49ms'. All dependencies present: anthropic, pydantic, httpx, etc. No package failures. Q36 (Will smoke test complete successfully?): RESOLVED ✓. Answer: YES. Smoke test completed successfully. Output: 'Found 55 turns'. No ImportError, ValueError, or FileNotFoundError. Predicted success 88% → actual 100%. Q37 (Will run_projection execute without errors?): PENDING. Still awaiting Turn 154+ execution. Confidence increased to ~90% (from 85% in Cycle 50). Will be resolved after run_projection command completes. Q31 (__init__.py verification status): RESOLVED ✓ (via smoke test confirmation). No ImportError means exports are correct. Q32 (log_reader smoke test): RESOLVED ✓ (Turn 153 output: 55 turns parsed). log_reader.py is working correctly. Q33 (What tensor output structure should run_projection produce?): PENDING. Will be confirmed once run_projection output is available (Turn 154+). Expected: JSON with strands, epistemic, declared_losses, open_questions. Q34 (Validate projector working, check structure/content): PENDING. Deferred to post-run_projection validation (Turns 157+). NEWLY OPENED QUESTIONS (Cycle 51): Q38: Given 55-turn baseline_run1 conversation (complex, tool_use/result nested), what strand count should projector produce? Too few (<3): risk of over-compression. Too many (>20): risk of poor semantic grouping. Q39: After 55 turns compressed into strands, what should average strand length be? Estimate: 500-2000 tokens per strand (balance between informativeness and compression). Q40: Should run_projection process ALL session files in baseline_run1/session/ or just the one tested (d5da3c7a...)? Cycle 49 plan said 'session directory' (plural files). Confirm scope.

key_claims.text: Q35 (uv sync success): RESOLVED ✓. YES, uv sync succeeded. 17 packages installed, zero errors.
Q36 (smoke test success): RESOLVED ✓. YES, smoke test succeeded. 55 turns parsed, no errors. Predicted 88% → actual 100%.
Q37 (run_projection success): PENDING. Confidence increased to ~90%. Will resolve Turn 154+.
Q38 (strand count for 55-turn conversation): Newly opened. Estimate: 3-20 strands. Too few = over-compression, too many = poor grouping.

strands.title: CYCLE 51 PREDICTION ACCURACY: Cycle 50 Forecasts vs. Cycle 51 Reality
strands.content: CYCLE 50 PREDICTIONS (made in Turn 150-level context): 'Turn 151 will deliver uv sync result. Success likelihood: 92%.' CYCLE 51 REALITY: Turn 151 DELIVERED uv sync result. SUCCESS = 100%. Prediction was CONSERVATIVE. 'Turns 152-153: Smoke test execution. Success likelihood: 88%. Expected output: Parsed N turns with N > 0.' CYCLE 51 REALITY: Turns 152-153 DELIVERED smoke test execution. SUCCESS = 100%. Output: 'Found 55 turns' with first 6 turns displayed. N = 55 (N > 0 confirmed). Prediction was CONSERVATIVE (by 12 percentage points). 'Turns 154+: run_projection execution. Success likelihood: 85%. Will produce tensor JSON files and statistics.' CYCLE 51 STATUS: Awaiting Turn 154+ execution. Confidence updated to ~90% (from 85%) based on validation of prior predictions. PREDICTION PATTERN: Cycle 50 made probabilistic forecasts. Cycle 51 shows reality trending toward or exceeding those forecasts. This suggests: (1) Cycle 50 analysis was sound but CONSERVATIVE, (2) Code quality and dependency management is BETTER than forecasted, (3) Baseline session data is WELL-FORMED and accessible. IMPLICATION FOR BLOCKER 3: Given 12-15 percentage point over-performance on Blockers 2a-2c, confidence in run_projection success should increase. Updated estimate: 88-92% (was 85%). LEARNING: Cycle projections benefit from conservative probability estimates (leaves room for positive surprises), but actual execution is exceeding forecast. This pattern suggests PROJECT HEALTH is GOOD.

key_claims.text: Cycle 50 predicted uv sync success 92%. Cycle 51 actual: 100%. Conservative estimate exceeded by 8 percentage points.
Cycle 50 predicted smoke test success 88%. Cycle 51 actual: 100%. Conservative estimate exceeded by 12 percentage points.
Confidence in run_projection success updated from 85% to 88-92%. Prior predictions were conservative. Project health is GOOD.

strands.title: CYCLE 51 NEXT PHASE INSTRUCTIONS: Blocker 3 Execution + Tensor Validation
strands.content: CYCLE 51 STATUS (END): Blocker 2 RESOLVED ✓. Blocker 3 READY TO COMMENCE. Session data validated (55 turns, parseable, rich content). Dependencies installed (17 packages, zero failures). IMMEDIATE NEXT ACTIONS (TURNS 154-158): TURN 154 (run_projection execution): Command: cd /home/tony/projects/hamutay && python -m hamutay.run_projection /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/*.jsonl /tmp/hamutay_output/ --max-turns 1000 Tool_use (Bash) to execute. Expected output: (A) Files processed count, (B) Total turns parsed, (C) Tensor cycles generated, (D) Output file paths. If ERROR: Likely I/O (output dir permissions), tensor initialization, or projector logic. Error message will diagnose. Fix and re-run. If SUCCESS: Proceed to Turn 155. TURN 155 (tensor output inspection): List output files: ls -lh /tmp/hamutay_output/ | grep .json Expected: 1+ JSON files written. If ZERO files: tensor initialization failed (silent failure risk). Diagnose and retry. If 1+ files: Proceed to Turn 156. TURN 156 (first tensor validation): Read first tensor file: cat /tmp/hamutay_output/[FIRST_FILE].json (or head -200 if large). Validate structure: (A) Is JSON parseable (valid syntax)? (B) Does it have keys: strands, declared_losses, open_questions, instructions_for_next, epistemic? (C) Are epistemic values numeric in [0,1]? (D) Do strands have title, content, key_claims? If ANY validation fails: tensor generation has bug. Error diagnosis from structure inspection. Fix projector logic, re-run. If ALL validations pass: Proceed to Turn 157. TURN 157 (semantic validation): (A) Are strands non-empty (N > 0)? Estimate N from file output. (B) Does first strand have meaningful title? Check first 50 chars. (C) Are key_claims present in strands (non-empty array)? (D) Do open_questions carry forward from baseline_run1 conversation topics? (E) Check compression: estimate input size (55 turns, ~500-2000 chars per turn = 27.5K-110K input), compare to strand content size. Is compression >50%? If compressions <50%: strands may be overly detailed or duplicative. If compression >90%: strands may lack detail. Ideal: 60-80% compression. If semantic validation passes: Tensor projection is WORKING and VALID. Proceed to Turn 158 (summary). TURN 158 (blocker 3 resolution + summary): Declare BLOCKER 3 = RESOLVED ✓. Summary: 'Hamutay baseline validation complete. Blockers 1, 2, 3 all RESOLVED. run_projection executed successfully on 55-turn baseline_run1 session. Tensor files generated with [N] strands, epistemic statistics [T/I/F values].' Recommendation: Hamutay ready for integration with Pichay eval pipeline. Next steps: (A) Document baseline results (metrics, compression ratio, strand topics), (B) Test on multiple baseline_run* sessions (if available), (C) Integrate run_projection into Pichay main pipeline. FAILURE RECOVERY (if Turns 154-157 hit errors): Diagnose error type, apply fix, re-run the failing step. Expected re-run turn budget: 2-4 turns per failure. Maximum total budget for Blocker 3 (including re-runs): 10 turns. CRITICAL SEQUENCING: Turn 154 (execution) → Turn 155 (file check) → Turn 156 (structure validation) → Turn 157 (semantic validation) → Turn 158 (summary). Do NOT skip steps.

key_claims.text: Blocker 3 activation ready. run_projection execution command prepared for Turn 154. No additional prerequisites.
Validation sequence (Turns 155-157): File check → Structure validation → Semantic validation. BLOCKER 3 = RESOLVED when all pass.
Expected outcomes: run_projection succeeds (90% confidence), produces 1+ tensor JSON files, strands with 60-80% compression ratio.
